_note: Fictional demo profile used to let first-time users see a populated app in one click. Not a real person.

entries.title: Robotics Software Engineer
entries.refinedNarrative: I own the motion-planning stack for warehouse autonomous mobile robots. I built the ROS2 planning nodes in C++ and Python, and profiling work I led cut planner latency by 35%. I also contributed to the fleet telemetry pipeline that surfaces failures across the deployment.
keyHighlights: Built ROS2 motion-planning nodes in C++/Python

entries.title: Embedded Engineer
entries.refinedNarrative: I wrote firmware for consumer drones: sensor-fusion routines, failsafe logic, and an over-the-air update system that shipped to roughly 200k devices. I supported the failsafe certification testing effort.
keyHighlights: Shipped sensor-fusion firmware for consumer drones
Designed an OTA update system reaching ~200k devices

entries.title: B.S. Computer Engineering
entries.refinedNarrative: Focused on embedded systems and control theory. Capstone project was an autonomous rover with vision-based navigation.

entries.title: OpenSwarm (open source)
entries.refinedNarrative: I maintain a multi-robot simulation library in Python with 800+ GitHub stars. I built the physics-accurate swarm simulator and review community pull requests each week.
keyHighlights: Maintainer of a Python swarm simulator, 800+ stars

entries.title: ML Study Group Lead
entries.refinedNarrative: I run a weekly applied-ML study group and built the PyTorch training pipelines we use on rented GPUs for hands-on sessions.
keyHighlights: Run a weekly applied-ML study group
Built PyTorch training pipelines on rented GPUs

goals.visionText: Build robots that do useful physical work. Stay hands-on with systems; avoid pure management for now.
goals.limitsText: No more than 20% travel. Remote-friendly teams only.
goals.identityText: A systems engineer who likes owning problems end-to-end, from firmware to fleet.

analyses.content:
## Skill clusters

- **Robotics core** — Motion Planning, ROS2, Robotics Simulation. Deepest cluster, evidenced by "Robotics Software Engineer" and "OpenSwarm".
- **Embedded foundations** — Embedded Firmware, Sensor Fusion, Control Theory ("Embedded Engineer", capstone).
- **Emerging ML thread** — ML Training Pipelines, PyTorch ("ML Study Group Lead") — recent but thin.

## Strengths

- Production robotics with a measurable result (35% planner latency reduction).
- A rare pairing: shipped firmware to ~200k devices **and** maintains a popular open-source simulator.

## Gaps & thin spots

- ML evidence is study-group level, not production. One deployed ML feature would change this materially.

## Suggested directions

1. **Well-evidenced now**: Senior robotics software roles.
2. **Needs development**: ML infrastructure for robotics — bridge the two strong clusters with one production ML project.
